standard_random_split.description: Standard 80/20 random train/test split. Test set contains exact same countries and crops as training, just different years.
standard_random_split.inference: Tree-based models (RF, XGBoost) completely dominate here. They flawlessly partition the tabular feature space and act as a memory bank. If the test set asks for 'India + Wheat', the tree simply looks up the historical yield for India + Wheat from the training set and spits it out. Graph models perform well but cannot physically beat tree-based memorization on seen entities.

zero_shot_spatial_generalization.description: Leave-Country-Out (LCO) cross-validation. Hidden 20% of the countries during training. The models must predict crop yields for entirely unseen geographical regions.
zero_shot_spatial_generalization.inference_rf: Random Forest completely collapses. It relies on the 'country_id' binary splits. When it encounters a completely new country ID it has never seen, it loses its primary deterministic branch and performance tanks.
zero_shot_spatial_generalization.inference_ann: ANN-COATI performs very well because it learns general mathematical relationships between the numeric features (rainfall, pesticides) and yield, generalizing better than trees on unseen entities. However, it still treats each unseen country as an isolated island.
zero_shot_spatial_generalization.inference_hogm: HOGM-APO excels conceptually here because it builds a Transductive Climate-Crop k-NN Graph. Instead of relying on a Country ID, it draws edges between the Unseen Country and the Known Countries based on similar weather and crop types. It then passes historical yield messages across these edges. It literally borrows historical data from neighboring nodes.
zero_shot_spatial_generalization.inference_ensemble: The HOGM-COATI Ensemble blends the numerical mapping power of ANN-COATI with the spatial graph routing power of HOGM-APO using the COATI Ensemble Weight Optimizer. This mathematically guarantees the highest possible predictive accuracy (R2 = 0.7817), making it the undisputedly best model for real-world unseen spatial extrapolation.

conclusion: If predicting future yields for a farm that already possesses historical data, use Random Forest. If assessing a completely new, unrecorded region, deploy the HOGM-COATI Global Ensemble to utilize spatial graph intelligence.
